surface.name: Cerebras Inference Quickstart via cURL
surface.selection_basis: The Cerebras Inference Quickstart states you can call the API directly with cURL and documents curl https://api.cerebras.ai/v1/chat/completions with Authorization: Bearer ${CEREBRAS_API_KEY}. SDK install is explicitly optional.

alternatives_considered.name: Python SDK
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Optional; the Quickstart says you can also call the API directly with cURL.

alternatives_considered.name: Developer Playground
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: In-browser option before a programmatic API call.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a Cerebras API key and complete a chat-completions request over HTTPS without installing an SDK.
assumed_prior_state: No Cerebras account, project, or API key is assumed.
A terminal with cURL is available; no language SDK is assumed.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: An authenticated cURL POST to https://api.cerebras.ai/v1/chat/completions returns a chat-completion message.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The response includes assistant content answering Why is fast inference important?.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Make your first API request includes a cURL example and shows expected response text.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The Quickstart names this the first API request and documents cURL as a first-class path. Free accounts can call the API with lower rate limits; paid upgrades are not required for first success.

prerequisites.requirement: A Cerebras account created through the Cloud Console; public docs do not enumerate the registration inputs or transitions.

Be at least 13 or the minimum age of digital consent required in the developer's country, provide accurate current account information, and accept the Website Terms of Use by accessing or using Cerebras Cloud.
An API key generated from API Keys in the Cloud Console and stored as `CEREBRAS_API_KEY`.
A terminal with cURL (or any HTTP client). No language SDK is required.

primary_path.action: Open the official Cerebras Inference Quickstart.
primary_path.output: The account, key, SDK, and first-request sequence is visible.
primary_path.success_signal: The `Make your first API request` section is available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No navigation failure or recovery behavior is documented.

primary_path.action: Sign up for Cerebras Cloud using the official Cloud Console.
details: Treat signup as one opaque prerequisite under the workshop policy.
primary_path.output: An authenticated Cerebras Cloud account is available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Signup fields are not publicly enumerated.

primary_path.action: Open API Keys in the left navigation, create a key, and copy it.
primary_path.output: A Cerebras API key is available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Paid accounts can require project selection.

primary_path.action: Export the key as `CEREBRAS_API_KEY` and run `echo $CEREBRAS_API_KEY`.
primary_path.output: The key is present in the shell.
primary_path.success_signal: The echo command prints the value.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Set it in the same shell that runs the code.

primary_path.action: Save the Quickstart's Python chat-completion example.
details: Use model `gpt-oss-120b` and the published prompt.
primary_path.success_signal: The script prints the first choice's message content.

primary_path.action: POST https://api.cerebras.ai/v1/chat/completions with cURL using Authorization: Bearer ${CEREBRAS_API_KEY}.
details: Send model gpt-oss-120b and the quickstart user message Why is fast inference important?.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: 401 means the key is unset; 429 means rate limit on free accounts.

primary_path.action: Confirm the chat-completion message text in the JSON response.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An error body means the call failed.

branches.path: Install `@cerebras/cerebras_cloud_sdk@latest`, initialize Cerebras with the environment key, run the published request, and print the first message content.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes the local runtime while preserving the same model, prompt, and printed-response terminal.

branches.path: Send the published POST to `/v1/chat/completions` with the bearer key, model, and user message.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Avoids SDK installation while preserving the same API response boundary.

branches.path: Select a project before generating the key so project limits and usage tracking apply.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds project selection; the free-trial path does not document this requirement.

branches.condition: The developer prefers the Python or Node SDK tab.
branches.path: Install the selected SDK and run the published example against the same chat-completions endpoint.

friction_gates.description: Required account registration is presented only through the Cloud Console application and is not enumerated in public documentation.
Cerebras requires the account holder to meet the applicable minimum age, provide accurate current information, and accept the Website Terms of Use through access or use.
API keys do not expire; Cerebras directs developers to store them in a secrets manager and rotate them periodically.
Free accounts have lower per-minute limits, and a `429 Too Many Requests` response means the limit was reached.

time_to_first_success.value: The Quickstart says the first Cerebras API call can be made in just minutes; it does not publish a measured signup-to-response duration.

sources.title: Cerebras Cloud
evidence_supported: Client application shell without a public static signup contract

sources.title: Website Terms of Use
evidence_supported: Cerebras Systems Inc. ownership of the cloud and inference services

uncertainties.question: What fields, authentication alternatives, agreements, verification actions, and submission states appear in current Cerebras Cloud signup?
uncertainties.impact: The required account path cannot be reconstructed or counted.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Official docs say only to sign up or log in, and the public Cloud Console response does not expose a static registration contract.

uncertainties.question: Does the $5 free-credit account require a payment method before the first request?
uncertainties.impact: A possible billing gate before API use is not resolved.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The pricing page states that credits arrive after account creation but does not state whether a payment method is required for the free trial.

excluded_after_success: Streaming, tool calling, structured outputs, reasoning, model selection, integrations, paid tiers, monitoring, and production design are outside the blocked selected path.
